walkthrough.title: Place the Basecamp Board
walkthrough.text: Let's get Expeditions set up. First, place the Basecamp board in the center of the table. This is the large board with the glory track printed on it, five card slots for face-up discovery cards, and space for the locations. The Basecamp itself is where all mechs begin the game, parked right next to the board.

walkthrough.title: Lay Out the Location Tiles
walkthrough.text: Find the 20 location tiles. They are divided into three regions: South, Central, and North. Separate them by region using the markings on their backs. Shuffle the 6 South tiles and place them face up in their designated positions on the board. Then shuffle the 7 Central tiles and place them face down in their spots. Finally, shuffle the 7 North tiles and place them face down in their spots. Face-down tiles represent unexplored territory your mechs will discover during the game.

walkthrough.title: Place Map Tokens
walkthrough.text: Grab the map tokens. Place 1 map token face down on each of the 14 face-down locations, meaning all Central and North tiles get one. The South tiles are already face up, so they do not get map tokens. Put the remaining map tokens in a general supply pile nearby. When you move to a face-down location during the game, you will gain that map token and flip the tile.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Discovery Deck
walkthrough.text: Find all the discovery cards. There are three types: items, meteorites, and quests. Shuffle them all together into one single face-down deck. Then deal 5 cards face up into the designated card slots on the Basecamp board. Place the remaining deck face down nearby. These face-up cards are available for players to gain during the game via the Gather action.

walkthrough.title: Fill the Corruption Bag
walkthrough.text: Find all the corruption tokens. These are the round tokens with power (orange) or guile (turquoise) costs printed on them. Place all corruption tokens into the cloth bag except for the special 20-value corruption token. Set that one aside. It only enters the game when location 20 is revealed. Give the bag a good shake to mix them up.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the General Supply
walkthrough.text: Create a shared supply area that everyone can reach. Place the workers of all five colors in separate piles: red soldiers, blue engineers, green explorers, yellow merchants, and purple possessed workers. Also place the coins and any remaining map tokens in the supply. These are resources players will gain throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Deal Mech Mats
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the 5 mech mats and deal 1 randomly to each player. Each mech mat shows the player's action spaces (Move, Play, Gather, Refresh), tracks for power and guile, spaces for workers, and a unique mech ability printed at the bottom. Each player takes the matching mech miniature and snaps on the colored base plate matching their player color.

walkthrough.title: Deal Starting Cards
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all the character and companion starting cards. Deal 1 character card and its matching companion card to each player. These two cards form your starting hand and define your unique abilities for the entire game. The character card has an ability on its top half, and the companion card has a different ability. Both go into your hand, ready to be played during the game.

walkthrough.title: Distribute Player Components
walkthrough.text: Each player takes 4 glory tokens (stars) in their color and 1 action token. Place the action token on the Refresh space of your mech mat. Set your power and guile trackers both to zero on the mech mat tracks. Place your mech miniature next to the Basecamp board. You are ready to explore.

walkthrough.title: Choose First Player and Begin
walkthrough.text: Randomly determine who goes first. Give each player 2 player aid cards for quick reference during the game. Play proceeds clockwise from the first player. On your very first turn, you will shift your action token from Refresh to the blank space, which means you get to perform all three actions: Move, Play, and Gather, in any order. That is a powerful opening turn, so think carefully about where to move and what to play. Let's learn the rules.

bullets: Place the Basecamp board centrally with the glory track and 5 card slots visible
All mechs start adjacent to the Basecamp

South tiles: shuffle and place face up
Central and North tiles: shuffle and place face down (unexplored)

summary.title: Place Map Tokens

South tiles (face up) do not get map tokens
Remaining map tokens go in the general supply

Deal 5 cards face up to the Basecamp card slots
Place remaining deck face down nearby

summary.title: Fill the Corruption Bag

Set the 20-value token aside (placed on location 20 when revealed)
Corruption tokens have power (orange) or guile (turquoise) costs

bullets: Workers in 5 colors: red, blue, green, yellow, purple
Coins and extra map tokens in shared supply
Place within reach of all players

summary.title: Deal Mech Mats
bullets: Randomly deal 1 of 5 mech mats to each player
Take matching mech miniature and colored base plate
Each mat has a unique mech ability (e.g., Highlander, Lumberjack)

summary.title: Deal Starting Cards

These form your 2-card starting hand with unique abilities
Characters include Akiko, Gunter, Anna, Matthew, Olga, and more

summary.title: Distribute Player Components

Action token starts on the Refresh space of the mech mat

summary.title: Choose First Player and Begin

Each player gets 2 player aid cards
First turn: shift action token from Refresh to blank space = all 3 actions (Move, Play, Gather)

walkthrough.title: Turn Structure Overview
walkthrough.text: Expeditions is played in consecutive clockwise turns with no rounds. On your turn, you always start by shifting your action token one space on your mech mat. The token cycles through positions that determine which actions you take. After shifting, you either perform actions or take a Refresh turn. The game continues until someone places their 4th glory token, at which point all remaining players take one final turn.

walkthrough.title: The Action Token Cycle
walkthrough.text: Your action token sits on your mech mat and controls what you do each turn. When your token starts on the Refresh space, you shift it to the blank space and perform all three actions: Move, Play, and Gather, in any order you choose. On your next turn, you shift it to cover one of the three actions (Move, Play, or Gather). You must perform the actions that are NOT covered. Then on a future turn, you can shift it to the Refresh space to take a Refresh turn. The cycle continues throughout the game. This means you alternate between powerful three-action turns and two-action turns, with Refresh turns resetting your cards.

walkthrough.title: Action: Move
walkthrough.text: Move your mech to another unoccupied location within range. The default range is 1 to 3 locations. You cannot move to a location occupied by another player's mech, though you may move through occupied locations. The Basecamp is adjacent to 3 locations for your first move, but you cannot return to Basecamp after leaving it. If you move onto a face-down location, your movement ends there. You gain the map token from that tile, flip the location face up, and draw corruption tokens from the bag until their combined value meets or exceeds the corruption value printed on the location. Stack the corruption tokens on the location, covering the rightmost benefit.

walkthrough.title: Action: Play
walkthrough.text: Play 1 card from your hand and place it in your active row, which is to the right of your mech mat. When you play a card, you gain its core value, which is the resource shown at the upper left corner of the card, usually power or guile. You may also place a matching-color worker from your mech mat onto the card to activate the card's special ability, printed at the bottom. Each card can hold only 1 worker. Workers placed on cards are considered active and stay there until you Refresh. The active row is face-up and visible to all players.

walkthrough.title: Action: Gather
walkthrough.text: Gather lets you gain all exposed benefits at your mech's current location. Benefits are the icons printed on the location tile. If corruption tokens are covering some benefits, you can only gain the ones that are still exposed. You must gain all exposed benefits if possible. A slash between benefits means you choose one or the other, not both. Benefits include gaining workers, gaining face-up cards from adjacent locations, gaining map tokens, and many special actions like Solve, Meld, Upgrade, Vanquish, Boast, Sweep, Rescue, and Refresh.

walkthrough.title: Action: Refresh
walkthrough.text: When you take a Refresh turn, return all cards from your active row back to your hand, and return all workers on those cards back to your mech mat. Your action token moves to the Refresh space. On your next turn, you shift the token to the blank space and get all three actions again. Refreshing is essential because your played cards sit in your active row, unavailable to play again until you refresh them. Time your Refresh turns wisely. You want to play as many cards as possible before refreshing to maximize the value of each cycle. Note: if a card or location grants a Refresh benefit, you return cards and workers but do NOT move your action token to Refresh.

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Items
walkthrough.text: Item cards have two types of abilities. Instant benefits are printed above the worker space and resolve immediately when you play the card. Ongoing abilities are active as long as the item is in your active row with a worker on it. The ongoing ability deactivates when you Refresh. Items can also be Upgraded: tuck the item under the right side of your mech mat so the ability is showing but the worker space is covered. Upgraded items keep their ongoing ability permanently active without needing a worker. You may upgrade up to 4 items during the game. Each upgraded item also has an end-game coin value printed on it.

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Quests
walkthrough.text: Quest cards show a specific location number. To solve a quest, your mech must be at the matching location. Pay the quest's Solve cost (shown on the right side of the card) to gain its Solve benefit (shown at the bottom). Solved quests are tucked above your mech mat. You may solve up to 4 quests during the game. The number of quests you have solved determines the value of each of your glory tokens at the end of the game: 0 quests = $5 per star, 1 quest = $6, 2 quests = $8, 3+ quests = $10 per star. Solving quests is one of the most important scoring multipliers.

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Meteorites
walkthrough.text: Meteorite cards can be melded. To meld a meteorite, tuck it under the bottom of your mech mat so only its meld bonus is visible. When you meld, you immediately gain the meld bonus of ALL melded meteorites, including the one you just melded. This creates a compounding effect: your first meld gives you 1 bonus, your second meld gives you 2 bonuses, your third gives you 3, and your fourth gives you 4. You may meld up to 4 meteorites during the game. Melded meteorites also increase the core value of meteorite cards you encounter later.

walkthrough.title: Workers and Card Abilities
walkthrough.text: Workers come in five colors: red soldiers, blue engineers, green explorers, yellow merchants, and purple possessed. When you play a card, you can place a matching-color worker from your mech mat onto the card to activate its ability. The worker must match the color icon on the bottom-left corner of the card. Workers stay on cards until you Refresh, at which point they return to your mech mat. Gaining more workers expands your ability to activate cards each cycle. You start with a small number and gain more through location benefits and card abilities.

walkthrough.title: Vanquishing Corruption
walkthrough.text: Corruption tokens sit on locations and cover benefits. To vanquish corruption, you use the Vanquish ability (gained from location benefits or card abilities). Pay the cost shown on the topmost corruption token at your location. Orange tokens cost power, and turquoise tokens cost guile. The amount varies by token. Once paid, you gain the corruption token and place it on your mech mat. You can vanquish multiple tokens in a single Vanquish action by paying each cost individually. When you remove the last corruption from a location, the previously covered benefit is revealed and becomes available through Gather. Each corruption token you collect is worth $2 at the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: Boasting and Glory
walkthrough.text: Boast is a special action gained through location benefits. When you Boast, place one of your 4 glory tokens on a glory category on the Basecamp board, but only if you currently meet that category's requirement and no one else has already placed a star there. The 8 glory categories are: Solve 4 quests, Meld 4 meteorites, Upgrade 4 items, Vanquish the 20-value corruption token, Collect 7 or more corruption tokens, Control 8 or more cards in hand and active row, Possess 7 or more workers, and Collect 5 or more map tokens. When a player places their 4th glory token, the game end is triggered.

walkthrough.title: Game End and Scoring
walkthrough.text: When someone places their 4th glory token, every other player takes one final turn, and then the game ends. Final scoring has four components. First, count all coins you gained during the game. Second, each glory token is worth $5, $6, $8, or $10 depending on the number of quests you solved (0, 1, 2, or 3+). Third, each corruption token you collected is worth $2. Fourth, add the coin value printed on each of your upgraded items. The player with the most total coins wins. In case of a tie, add up all progress across glory categories. If still tied, share the victory.

bullets: No rounds — continuous clockwise turns until game end

Game ends when a player places their 4th glory token (others get 1 final turn)

summary.title: The Action Token Cycle
bullets: Refresh space -> blank space = all 3 actions (Move, Play, Gather) in any order
Blank space -> cover 1 action = perform the 2 uncovered actions
Covering Move/Play/Gather -> Refresh space = take a Refresh turn
Cycle: big turn (3 actions), medium turn (2 actions), Refresh, repeat

summary.title: Action: Move
bullets: Move your mech to an unoccupied location within range (default 1-3)

Optionally place a matching-color worker to activate the card's ability
Each card holds at most 1 worker; cards stay active until Refresh

bullets: Gain all exposed benefits at your mech's current location
Corruption tokens cover some benefits — only gain what is exposed
Slash (/) between benefits = choose one, not both
Benefits include: workers, cards, Solve, Meld, Upgrade, Vanquish, Boast, Sweep, Rescue

bullets: Return all active row cards to hand, all workers to mech mat

Refresh benefits from cards do NOT move the action token

Solving quests is the most powerful scoring multiplier

When melding, gain meld bonus of ALL melded meteorites (compounds each time)
1st meld = 1 bonus, 2nd = 2 bonuses, 3rd = 3, 4th = 4 (total 10 bonuses)
Melded meteorites increase future meteorite core values

summary.title: Workers and Card Abilities
bullets: 5 worker colors: red (soldiers), blue (engineers), green (explorers), yellow (merchants), purple (possessed)
Place matching-color worker on a played card to activate its ability
Workers stay on cards until Refresh returns them to mech mat
More workers = more abilities activated per cycle

Each glory token: $5/$6/$8/$10 based on 0/1/2/3+ quests solved
Each corruption token collected: $2
Coin value on upgraded items
Tiebreaker: total progress across all glory categories

walkthrough.title: Welcome and Meet the Players
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first cycle of turns with three example players: Player 1, Player 2, and Player 3. Three players is the recommended count for Expeditions. Each player has a different mech and character/companion pair, and we will demonstrate the core action loop, common mistakes, and how to start building your engine. Follow along even if you have more or fewer players.

walkthrough.title: Starting Position
walkthrough.text: All three mechs sit next to the Basecamp. Each player has their action token on the Refresh space of their mech mat, meaning their first turn will be the powerful three-action turn. Each player holds just 2 cards: their character and companion. Power and guile are both at zero. The 6 South locations are face up, and 5 discovery cards are displayed on the Basecamp. The Central and North tiles are still face down with map tokens on them. Time to explore.

walkthrough.title: Player 1's First Turn: All Three Actions
walkthrough.text: Player 1 shifts the action token from Refresh to the blank space, unlocking all three actions: Move, Play, and Gather, in any order. First, Player 1 Moves to a revealed South location that has an exposed worker benefit. Then Player 1 Plays their character card from hand, placing it in the active row to gain 1 power from its core value. Finally, Player 1 Gathers, gaining a green worker from the location's exposed benefit and placing it on the mech mat. Great first turn. Player 1 now has 1 power, 1 new worker, and 1 card in the active row.

walkthrough.title: Why That Was a Smart Opening
walkthrough.text: Player 1 made three good decisions. Moving to a location with an accessible worker benefit was smart because workers are the fuel that powers card abilities. Playing a card immediately started generating resources. And Gathering a worker expanded future options. In Expeditions, your first few turns are about collecting workers and resources so you can activate card abilities in later cycles.

walkthrough.title: Player 2's First Turn: All Three Actions
walkthrough.text: Player 2 also shifts from Refresh to the blank space for all three actions. Player 2 Moves to a different South location near a face-up discovery card. Then Player 2 Gathers first this time, picking up a face-up item card from an adjacent location. The item goes into the active row. Player 2 also Plays their companion card, placing a red worker on it to activate the companion's ability, which lets them Vanquish. Player 2 pays 2 power to vanquish the top corruption token at their location, gaining it.

walkthrough.title: Player 2's Clever Sequencing
walkthrough.text: Notice that Player 2 chose the order of actions carefully. By Gathering first, Player 2 gained an item card into the active row. Then by Playing the companion card with a worker to Vanquish, Player 2 removed corruption and revealed a benefit for future Gathers. The order of Move, Play, and Gather matters a lot. Think about which sequence gives you the most value each turn.

walkthrough.title: Player 3's First Turn: The Mistake
walkthrough.text: Player 3 shifts from Refresh to the blank space. Player 3 Moves to a South location. Then Player 3 Plays their character card but does NOT place a worker on it. They just take the core value of 1 guile. Then Player 3 Gathers but the location's benefits are mostly covered by corruption. Player 3 gets a single map token. Player 3 ends the turn with 1 guile, 1 map token, 1 card in the active row, and zero workers gained. Not a disaster, but much weaker than Players 1 and 2.

walkthrough.title: Mistake Analysis: Ignoring Workers
walkthrough.text: Player 3's main error was not prioritizing workers early. Without new workers, Player 3 could not activate card abilities beyond the core value. The core value alone (1 power or 1 guile per card) is useful but slow. Placing workers on cards to trigger abilities is where the real power of the game comes from. In Expeditions, your first priority should be gaining workers so you can activate more abilities each cycle.

walkthrough.title: Player 1's Second Turn: Cover One Action
walkthrough.text: Player 1 now shifts the action token from the blank space to cover one of the three actions. Player 1 covers Move, meaning they will Play and Gather this turn but not Move. Player 1 Plays their companion card, placing a blue worker on it to activate the ability, which gains a face-up quest card. Then Player 1 Gathers at their location, gaining 1 coin from an exposed benefit. Player 1 is building up cards and resources without moving.

walkthrough.title: Player 2's Second Turn: Exploring North
walkthrough.text: Player 2 shifts to cover Gather, taking Move and Play this turn. Player 2 Moves to a face-down Central location. Movement ends there immediately. Player 2 gains the map token, flips the tile face up, and draws corruption tokens from the bag. Two corruption tokens come out, covering the rightmost benefit on the new location. Player 2 then Plays a card for its core value. Player 2 has now explored new territory, setting up future Gathers once the corruption is cleared.

walkthrough.title: Player 3's Second Turn: Another Mistake
walkthrough.text: Player 3 shifts to cover Play, taking Move and Gather. Player 3 Moves to a face-down Central location, revealing it and gaining a map token. Corruption tokens are drawn. Then Player 3 tries to Gather, but both benefits on this new location are covered by corruption tokens. Player 3 has nothing to Gather. The turn is effectively wasted because Player 3 moved to a location without checking whether any benefits would be accessible.

walkthrough.title: Mistake Analysis: Moving Without a Plan
walkthrough.text: Player 3 moved to a new location without considering that newly revealed locations will have corruption covering their benefits. The Gather action is wasted if there is nothing exposed to gain. Before moving to a face-down location, think about whether you will be able to Gather anything useful there. Newly revealed tiles almost always start with corruption, so you may want to Vanquish first or move to an already-cleared location instead.

walkthrough.title: Player 1 and Player 2: Refresh Turns
walkthrough.text: Both Player 1 and Player 2 now shift their action tokens to the Refresh space. They return all cards from their active rows to their hands and return all workers from those cards to their mech mats. This resets everything. Next turn, they will shift to the blank space again and get all three actions with a bigger hand of cards and more workers. Each Refresh cycle makes them stronger because they have gained new cards and workers since the last one.

walkthrough.title: The Engine Building Loop
walkthrough.text: This is the core engine of Expeditions. Play cards to gain resources, place workers to activate abilities, Gather benefits at locations, then Refresh to get everything back. Each cycle, you have more cards in hand and more workers on your mat, so each subsequent cycle is more powerful than the last. The players who gain workers early and Refresh at the right time will build stronger engines and score more points.

walkthrough.title: Player 3's Recovery: Prioritizing Workers
walkthrough.text: Player 3 learns from the earlier mistakes. On the next big turn, Player 3 Moves to a South location with an exposed worker benefit. Player 3 Plays a card with a worker this time, activating the character ability to gain a Solve benefit. Player 3 Gathers, gaining a yellow worker from the location. Now Player 3 has 2 workers and can activate abilities on both cards next cycle. The engine is starting to turn.

walkthrough.title: Mistake Analysis: Forgetting to Vanquish
walkthrough.text: Player 3 also made a subtle error across the first few turns: ignoring corruption entirely. Corruption tokens cover location benefits, blocking your Gather action. More importantly, each corruption token is worth $2 at end of game. Players who vanquish early not only unlock better Gather benefits but also accumulate scoring tokens. Do not leave corruption sitting on locations you visit frequently. Build up power and guile, then Vanquish to both score points and open up better benefits.

walkthrough.title: Glory Token Strategy Preview
walkthrough.text: As the game progresses, watch the 8 glory categories on the Basecamp board. Only one star can occupy each category, so there is a race element. If you see you are close to 7 workers or 5 map tokens, push to Boast before someone else claims that spot. Remember that each glory token's value depends on how many quests you have solved, so solving quests early amplifies the value of every star you place. The player who places their 4th star triggers the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have now seen all four action types, watched Player 3 learn from three common mistakes, and understood the core engine-building loop. The key takeaways: gain workers early, plan your moves before committing, vanquish corruption for points and better Gather benefits, and time your Refresh turns to maximize each cycle. You are ready to continue playing on your own.

walkthrough.title: Before You Explore
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into a full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to every action, card type, and game term.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun exploring!

summary.title: Before You Explore
bullets: Check out General Tips for strategy basics
Reference the Appendix for every action, card type, and game term
Ask your personal GM Guide in the Q&A tab for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before we get into strategy, here are the biggest mistakes new players make in Expeditions. First, not gaining workers early, which severely limits your ability to activate card abilities. Second, moving to face-down locations without a plan for dealing with corruption. Third, hoarding cards in hand without playing them, which means their core values and abilities are never used. Fourth, ignoring quest solving, which causes your glory tokens to be worth only $5 each instead of up to $10. Fifth, forgetting that you must gain all exposed benefits when you Gather. Keep these in mind and you will play a much stronger game from the start.

walkthrough.title: First Turn Priorities
walkthrough.text: Your very first turn gives you all three actions. Make it count. The most important thing is to move to a location where you can gain a worker. Workers let you activate card abilities, and abilities are far more powerful than just core values. A strong opening is: Move to a South location with an exposed worker benefit, Play your character or companion card (gaining power or guile), and Gather the worker. By the end of turn one, you should have at least one new worker on your mech mat.

walkthrough.title: Refresh Timing
walkthrough.text: Knowing when to Refresh is one of the most important skills in Expeditions. You want to play as many cards as possible before Refreshing because each Refresh costs you a turn. If you have 4 cards in hand, try to play all 4 across your action turns before Refreshing. If you Refresh with cards still in hand, you have left value on the table. On the other hand, do not delay Refreshing if you have already played all your cards. Sitting with an empty hand and nothing to Play is also wasted potential. The ideal rhythm is: big turn, action turn, action turn, Refresh.

walkthrough.title: Worker Economy
walkthrough.text: Workers are the engine fuel of Expeditions. Without workers, you can only gain core values from cards, which gives you 1 power or 1 guile per card. With workers, you unlock the card's special ability, which is almost always worth more than the core value. Early in the game, prioritize gaining workers from location benefits. By mid-game, you want 4 to 5 workers so you can activate most of your cards each cycle. Remember that workers return to your mech mat when you Refresh, so they are reusable every cycle.

walkthrough.title: The Quest Multiplier
walkthrough.text: Quests are the single most impactful scoring element in the game. Each glory token is worth $5 with no quests solved, $6 with 1, $8 with 2, and $10 with 3 or more. If you place all 4 glory tokens, that is $20 with no quests versus $40 with 3 quests solved. That is a 20-coin swing. Start looking for quests early. When you Gather a quest card, check its location requirement. Plan your movement so you can reach that location and Solve it. Solving 2 to 3 quests should be a baseline goal every game.

walkthrough.title: Corruption as a Resource
walkthrough.text: Many new players view corruption as an obstacle, but it is also a valuable resource. Each corruption token you vanquish is worth $2 at end of game. Collecting 7 or more corruption tokens also qualifies you for a glory category. Build up your power and guile tracks so you can afford to Vanquish. A player who vanquishes 8 corruption tokens over the course of the game earns $16 in points plus a potential glory token. Do not leave corruption sitting on locations you occupy. Clear it, score it, and unlock the hidden benefit.

walkthrough.title: Action Order Matters
walkthrough.text: When you have multiple actions on a turn, the order you perform them in can make a huge difference. For example, if you Gather first, you might pick up a new card from an adjacent location. Then you can Play that new card immediately on the same turn. Or, if you Play first and gain power from the core value, you might have enough power to Vanquish corruption during your Gather. Always think about which sequence gives you the most value before committing to your first action.

walkthrough.title: Glory Category Racing
walkthrough.text: Each of the 8 glory categories on the Basecamp board can only hold one star. This creates a race. If you are close to 7 workers, check whether someone else is also approaching that threshold. If they are, you may want to push to Boast before they do. On the other hand, if a category is uncontested, you can wait and pursue it later. Pay attention to what other players are building toward. Flexible players who can pivot to unclaimed categories will place more glory tokens than players who tunnel on one path and get blocked.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Not gaining workers early — limits card ability activation
Moving to face-down locations without a plan for corruption
Hoarding cards in hand without playing them (zero value)
Ignoring quests — drops glory token value from $10 to $5 each
Forgetting to gain all exposed benefits when Gathering

summary.title: First Turn Priorities
bullets: All 3 actions available on turn 1 — make it count
Move to a South location with an exposed worker benefit
Play a card for core value, Gather to gain a worker
End turn 1 with at least 1 new worker on your mat

summary.title: Refresh Timing
bullets: Play all cards from hand before Refreshing for maximum value
Do not Refresh with cards still unplayed in hand
Do not sit with an empty hand — Refresh when hand is empty
Ideal rhythm: big turn (3 actions), action turns, Refresh

summary.title: Worker Economy
bullets: Workers = engine fuel; without them, cards only give core value
Prioritize gaining workers from location benefits early
Target 4-5 workers by mid-game for full card activation
Workers are reusable — they return to mat every Refresh

summary.title: The Quest Multiplier
bullets: Glory token values: $5 (0 quests), $6 (1), $8 (2), $10 (3+)
4 glory tokens with 3 quests = $40 vs $20 with 0 quests
Solving 2-3 quests should be a baseline goal every game
Plan movement toward quest locations early

summary.title: Corruption as a Resource
bullets: Each corruption token = $2 at end of game
7+ corruption tokens qualifies for a glory category
Build power and guile to afford Vanquishing
Clearing corruption unlocks hidden location benefits

summary.title: Action Order Matters
bullets: Gather first to gain cards, then Play them on the same turn
Play first to gain power/guile, then Vanquish during Gather
Move last if you don't need positioning for other actions
Think through full action sequence before committing

summary.title: Glory Category Racing
bullets: Each category holds only 1 star — first to claim wins it
Watch what opponents are building toward
Push to Boast when close to a contested category
Flexible players who pivot to unclaimed categories place more stars

walkthrough.title: Quest-Heavy Strategy
walkthrough.text: The quest-heavy approach aims to solve 3 or 4 quests as fast as possible, maximizing the value of every glory token. With 3 quests solved, each star is worth $10 instead of $5, doubling your glory income. Start by Gathering quest cards early and checking their location requirements. Plan your movement path to hit multiple quest locations. Prioritize gaining the specific workers needed to activate Solve abilities. This strategy pairs naturally with the Solve 4 quests glory category. The downside is that quest locations may be scattered across the map, requiring more Move actions.

walkthrough.title: Corruption Farming Strategy
walkthrough.text: The corruption farming approach focuses on building high power and guile, then systematically Vanquishing corruption tokens across multiple locations. Each token is worth $2, and collecting 7 or more unlocks a glory category. A player who vanquishes 10 tokens earns $20 from corruption alone plus a glory star. This strategy works well with mechs like the Lumberjack, which reduces the cost of the first corruption of each color by 1. Focus on power and guile generation early, move to corruption-heavy locations, and Vanquish repeatedly. The 20-value corruption token on location 20 is an entire glory category by itself if you can afford the 10 combined power and guile to claim it.

walkthrough.title: Worker Accumulation Strategy
walkthrough.text: The worker accumulation approach targets the 7 workers glory category while building a powerful engine. More workers mean more card abilities activated each cycle, which compounds over time. Prioritize location benefits that grant workers of specific colors. By mid-game, with 5 to 6 workers, you can activate nearly every card you play. This generates large amounts of power, guile, and coins per cycle. The 7 workers category is one of the most contested in the game because workers are universally useful, so you may need to push for it quickly before another player claims it.

walkthrough.title: Meld Engine Strategy
walkthrough.text: The meld engine approach exploits the compounding nature of meteorite melding. Each time you meld, you gain the bonus of ALL melded meteorites. Your first meld gives 1 bonus, your second gives 2, third gives 3, and fourth gives 4. Over 4 melds, you receive a total of 10 bonuses. If those bonuses include power, guile, and coins, you can generate massive resource spikes. This strategy accelerates in the mid-game and pairs well with the Meld 4 meteorites glory category. Prioritize Gathering meteorite cards early and reaching locations that offer the Meld action.

walkthrough.title: Upgrade Engine Strategy
walkthrough.text: The upgrade engine focuses on upgrading 4 items to gain permanent ongoing abilities and end-game coin values. Upgraded items do not need workers and do not need to be Refreshed, making them permanently active. This frees up workers for other cards and reduces your dependence on Refresh timing. Look for items with strong ongoing abilities: things like gaining extra resources when you move, bonus actions when you Gather, or extra power/guile during specific actions. The coin values printed on upgraded items also contribute directly to your final score.

walkthrough.title: Map Token Collection Strategy
walkthrough.text: The map token strategy revolves around collecting 5 or more map tokens for the glory category while leveraging map-related abilities. You gain map tokens by moving to face-down locations and from certain location benefits. The Odin's Wrath mech ability lets you trash map tokens for 1 power, 1 guile, or $1, providing flexible resource conversion. This strategy involves aggressive exploration of the Central and North regions, flipping face-down tiles and collecting tokens. It pairs well with being the first player to explore new territory.

walkthrough.title: Card Control Strategy
walkthrough.text: The card control approach targets the 8 or more cards glory category by accumulating a large hand and active row. Cards you control includes both your hand and your active row, but not tucked cards (solved quests, upgraded items, melded meteorites). To build card count, Gather face-up discovery cards from locations frequently and avoid Refreshing until you have played many cards to your active row. The Highlander mech ability sends gained cards directly to hand instead of the active row, giving you more flexibility. This strategy benefits from visiting locations with multiple card-gaining benefits.

walkthrough.title: Mech Ability Synergies
walkthrough.text: Each mech ability shapes your optimal strategy. The Marsh Strider extends your Move range to 1-4, perfect for a quest-solving or exploration strategy that requires visiting many locations. The Lumberjack reduces Vanquish costs, making corruption farming significantly cheaper. Odin's Wrath converts map tokens into flexible resources, rewarding aggressive exploration. The Highlander sends gained cards to hand, supporting card accumulation and flexible play. Learn your mech's strength and build around it rather than fighting against it. The mech ability should inform your first three turns.

walkthrough.title: Timing the End Game
walkthrough.text: The player who places the 4th glory token controls the pace of the game. Everyone else gets only one final turn. This creates a powerful timing weapon. If you are ahead on scoring, rush to place your 4th star and end the game before opponents can catch up. If you are behind, consider blocking contested glory categories or delaying your own Boasts so other players cannot trigger the end. Watch the Basecamp board constantly. Count how many stars each player has placed. If someone has 3 stars and is approaching a 4th category, you may need to race for your own remaining categories or prepare for a high-scoring final turn.

walkthrough.title: Scoring Optimization
walkthrough.text: The highest-scoring players in Expeditions diversify their points across multiple sources. A typical winning score includes 20 to 30 coins from glory tokens (4 stars at $5-$10 each), 10 to 16 coins from corruption tokens, 5 to 15 coins from upgraded items, and accumulated coins from abilities and card plays throughout the game. The biggest lever is quest solving: going from 0 quests to 3 quests adds $20 to your glory token total alone. After quests, corruption is the next most efficient scoring source at $2 per token. Do not neglect either of these categories.

summary.title: Quest-Heavy Strategy
bullets: Solve 3+ quests to maximize glory token value ($10 per star vs $5)
4 stars with 3 quests = $40 vs $20 with 0 quests
Gather quest cards early and plan movement to matching locations
Pairs with Solve 4 quests glory category

summary.title: Corruption Farming Strategy
bullets: Each corruption token = $2; 10 tokens = $20 from corruption alone
7+ corruption = glory category; 20-value token = its own glory category
Lumberjack mech reduces Vanquish cost by 1 per color
Build power and guile early, then Vanquish repeatedly

summary.title: Worker Accumulation Strategy
bullets: 7+ workers = glory category + more abilities activated per cycle
More workers = stronger engine each Refresh cycle
Highly contested category — push for it quickly
Workers are reusable, so each one compounds over the game

summary.title: Meld Engine Strategy
bullets: Melding compounds: 1st meld = 1 bonus, 2nd = 2, 3rd = 3, 4th = 4 (total 10)
Meld bonuses can include power, guile, coins — massive resource spikes
Pairs with Meld 4 meteorites glory category
Prioritize Gathering meteorite cards early

summary.title: Upgrade Engine Strategy
bullets: Upgraded items = permanent ongoing abilities without workers or Refresh
Frees up workers for other cards each cycle
Upgraded items have end-game coin values contributing to score
Pairs with Upgrade 4 items glory category

summary.title: Map Token Collection Strategy
bullets: 5+ map tokens = glory category
Gained by exploring face-down locations and from location benefits
Odin's Wrath mech: trash maps for 1 power, 1 guile, or $1
Rewards aggressive exploration of Central and North regions

summary.title: Card Control Strategy
bullets: 8+ cards controlled (hand + active row) = glory category
Gather face-up discovery cards frequently to build card count
Highlander mech sends gained cards to hand for flexibility
Avoid Refreshing too early — keep active row cards counted

summary.title: Mech Ability Synergies
bullets: Marsh Strider (range 1-4): quest solving and exploration
Lumberjack (reduced Vanquish cost): corruption farming
Odin's Wrath (trash maps for resources): flexible economy
Highlander (cards to hand): card accumulation and flexibility

summary.title: Timing the End Game
bullets: 4th glory token = game end (others get 1 final turn)
If ahead: rush to end the game before opponents catch up
If behind: block contested categories or delay
Always count opponent stars and monitor their progress

summary.title: Scoring Optimization
bullets: Typical winning score: glory ($20-40) + corruption ($10-16) + upgrades ($5-15) + coins
Quest solving is the biggest lever: 0 to 3 quests = +$20 on glory alone
Corruption is next most efficient at $2 per token
Diversify scoring sources — do not tunnel on one category

entries.definition: One of the three main actions. Move your mech to another unoccupied location within range (default 1-3 locations). You may move through occupied locations but cannot end on one. Moving to a face-down location ends your movement, reveals the tile, and places corruption tokens on it.

One of the three main actions. Play 1 card from your hand to your active row, gaining its core value (power or guile). You may place a matching-color worker on the card to activate its special ability. Each card holds at most 1 worker.
One of the three main actions. Gain all exposed benefits at your mech's current location. You must gain all exposed benefits if possible. A slash between benefits means choose one.

Return all active row cards to your hand and all workers on those cards to your mech mat. When taken as a turn action, the action token shifts to the Refresh space. When gained as a benefit from a card or location, the action token does NOT shift.

A special action gained through location benefits or card abilities. Pay the cost of the topmost corruption token at your location to gain it. Orange tokens cost power; turquoise tokens cost guile. You may vanquish multiple tokens in a single Vanquish action.

A special action to complete a quest card. Your mech must be at the location matching the quest. Pay the quest's Solve cost to gain its Solve benefit. Solved quests are tucked above your mech mat. Maximum 4 quests solved per game.

A special action to tuck a meteorite card under the bottom of your mech mat. When you meld, you immediately gain the meld bonus of ALL melded meteorites, including the one just melded. Creates a compounding effect. Maximum 4 melds per game.

A special action to tuck an item card under the right side of your mech mat. The item's ongoing ability becomes permanently active without needing a worker. The worker space is covered. Maximum 4 upgrades per game. Upgraded items have an end-game coin value.

A special action gained through location benefits. Place one of your 4 glory tokens on a Basecamp glory category that you currently meet and that has no other star on it. Placing the 4th glory token triggers the end of the game.

A location benefit that lets you discard multiple face-up discovery cards from the Basecamp slots and replace them with new cards from the deck. Useful for cycling the card display to find cards you need.
A location benefit that returns an active row card to your hand and returns any worker on it to your mech mat. Unlike Refresh, Rescue targets only a single card.

A small token placed on your mech mat that determines which actions you perform each turn. It cycles through positions: Refresh, blank (all 3 actions), and covering one of Move/Play/Gather (2 actions). You shift it at the start of every turn.

Your personal player board showing action spaces (Move, Play, Gather, Refresh), power and guile tracks, worker storage, and a unique mech ability. The action token slides along the top of the mat. Cards are played to the active row beside the mat.
A large plastic miniature representing your mech on the map. Each of the 5 mechs has a unique sculpt. A colored base plate snaps onto the bottom to identify your player color.
The central board showing the glory track with 8 categories, 5 slots for face-up discovery cards, and the starting position for all mechs. Players place glory tokens here when they Boast.

20 modular hex tiles divided into South (6), Central (7), and North (7) regions. South tiles start face up; Central and North tiles start face down with map tokens. Each tile shows a corruption value, location number, and benefits.

Small tokens placed on face-down locations. When you move to a face-down location, you gain the map token and flip the tile. Collecting 5+ map tokens qualifies for a glory category. Extra map tokens are available from the supply.

Round tokens placed on revealed locations. They cover location benefits and must be Vanquished (by paying their power or guile cost) to remove. Orange tokens cost power; turquoise tokens cost guile. Each collected token is worth $2 at end of game.
A special corruption token placed on location 20 when that tile is revealed. It requires 10 combined power and guile to Vanquish. Claiming it qualifies for its own glory category.

A cloth bag holding all corruption tokens except the 20-value token. When a face-down location is revealed, corruption tokens are drawn from the bag until their combined value meets or exceeds the location's corruption value.

Star-shaped tokens in each player's color. Each player has 4. Placed on the Basecamp glory track when Boasting. Each star's value depends on quests solved: $5 (0 quests), $6 (1), $8 (2), $10 (3+). The 4th star placed triggers game end.
A single shuffled deck containing all item, meteorite, and quest cards. Five cards are displayed face up on the Basecamp board. When a card is taken, it is immediately replaced from the deck.
Reference cards given to each player summarizing the actions (Move, Play, Gather, Refresh), key rules, and iconography. Each player receives 2 player aid cards during setup.
The currency and victory points of Expeditions. Coins are gained through card abilities, location benefits, and end-game scoring. Your total coins at game end determine your final score and winner.

A type of discovery card with instant and/or ongoing abilities. Items can be played to the active row for their core value and abilities, or Upgraded by tucking under the mech mat for permanent effects. Upgraded items have an end-game coin value.

A type of discovery card tied to a specific location number. Quests are solved by moving to the matching location and paying the Solve cost to gain the Solve benefit. Solved quests tuck above the mech mat and multiply glory token values.

A type of discovery card that can be melded by tucking under the bottom of the mech mat. Melding triggers the meld bonus of ALL melded meteorites, creating a compounding effect. Melded meteorites also increase future meteorite core values.
One of each player's 2 starting cards. Characters have a unique ability on their top half and define one of the starting card pair. Characters include Akiko, Gunter, Anna, Matthew, Olga, and others.
The second of each player's 2 starting cards. Companions are animal partners that have a different ability from the character. Companions include Jiro, Nacht, Wojtek, Caesar, Changa, and others.

The resource shown at the upper left of a card. When you Play a card, you gain its core value (usually power or guile). Some core values are conditional, increasing based on your current game state such as number of melded meteorites or stars placed.

The area to the right of your mech mat where played cards are placed face up. Cards in the active row are considered under your control. Workers placed on active row cards stay there until Refresh. Active row cards with workers have their ongoing abilities active.
All cards in your hand and your active row. Does not include tucked cards (solved quests, upgraded items, melded meteorites). Relevant for the 8+ cards glory category.

One of the two main resources, tracked on the mech mat (maximum 10). Power is used to Vanquish orange corruption tokens and pay certain card costs. Gained from card core values, abilities, and location benefits. Shown as an orange/red icon.

One of the two main resources, tracked on the mech mat (maximum 10). Guile is used to Vanquish turquoise corruption tokens and pay certain card costs. Gained from card core values, abilities, and location benefits. Shown as a turquoise icon.
One of the 5 worker colors. Red workers are placed on cards with a red worker icon to activate their abilities. Gained from location benefits and card abilities.
One of the 5 worker colors. Blue workers activate cards with a blue worker icon. Gained from location benefits and card abilities.
One of the 5 worker colors. Green workers activate cards with a green worker icon. Gained from location benefits and card abilities.
One of the 5 worker colors. Yellow workers activate cards with a yellow worker icon. Gained from location benefits and card abilities.
One of the 5 worker colors. Purple workers activate cards with a purple worker icon. The rarest and most unusual worker type. Gained from location benefits and card abilities.

Being next to a location on the map. Adjacency always refers to your mech's current position, not cards in your active row. Adjacent benefits and adjacent card activations use the locations next to where your mech is standing.

An unexplored Central or North location tile. Moving onto it ends your movement. You gain the map token, flip the tile face up, and draw corruption tokens from the bag until their value meets or exceeds the location's corruption value.
A location benefit that is not covered by corruption tokens. When you Gather, you only gain exposed benefits. As corruption is vanquished, more benefits become exposed.
An item card benefit printed above the worker space that triggers immediately when the card is played. Instant benefits fire once and are not affected by Refresh.

An item card ability that remains active as long as the card is in the active row with a worker on it. The ability deactivates when the card is Refreshed. Upgraded items keep their ongoing ability permanently active.
The resource cost shown on the right side of a quest card. Must be paid to solve the quest. Common costs include power, guile, or combinations of resources.
The reward shown at the bottom of a quest card, gained when you successfully solve the quest. Benefits vary by quest and may include resources, workers, or coins.

The bonus shown at the bottom of a meteorite card when it is melded (tucked under the mech mat). When you meld any meteorite, you gain the meld bonus of ALL currently melded meteorites, creating a compounding effect.
The 6 location tiles closest to Basecamp. South tiles start face up with no map tokens and no corruption. These are the safest and most accessible locations at the start of the game.
The 7 location tiles in the middle of the map. Central tiles start face down with map tokens. They have moderate corruption values. Exploring Central locations is typically the mid-game focus.

The 7 location tiles farthest from Basecamp. North tiles start face down with map tokens and have the highest corruption values. Location 20 is in the North and holds the special 20-value corruption token.
Each of the 20 location tiles has a unique number. Quest cards reference these numbers to indicate where they can be solved. All 20 locations always appear in every game.

The number printed on a location tile indicating how much corruption must be placed when the tile is revealed. Corruption tokens are drawn from the bag until their combined value meets or exceeds this number.
Glory category. Requires having 4 solved quests tucked above your mech mat. One of the hardest categories to achieve but synergizes with maximizing glory token values.
Glory category. Requires having 4 melded meteorite cards tucked under the bottom of your mech mat.
Glory category. Requires having 4 upgraded item cards tucked under the right side of your mech mat.
Glory category. Requires having vanquished the special 20-value corruption token from location 20, which costs 10 combined power and guile.
Glory category. Requires having collected 7 or more corruption tokens through Vanquishing (not counting the 20-value token for this category).
Glory category. Requires controlling 8 or more cards in your hand and active row combined. Does not count tucked cards (upgrades, melds, solved quests).
Glory category. Requires having 7 or more workers on your mech mat and on your active row cards combined.
Glory category. Requires having collected 5 or more map tokens through exploration and location benefits.
Mech ability. Allows you to Solve, Upgrade, and Meld up to 5 times each instead of the default maximum of 4. Extends the ceiling for quest, item, and meteorite strategies.
Mech ability. Increases your Move range to 1-4 locations instead of the default 1-3. Enables faster exploration and easier access to distant quest locations.
Mech ability. You may trash a map token at any time on your turn to gain 1 power, 1 guile, or $1. Can be used multiple times per turn. Converts map tokens into flexible resources.
Mech ability. Whenever you Vanquish, reduce the cost of the first corruption of each color by 1. Makes corruption farming significantly cheaper. Example: a 4-cost orange token only costs 3 power.
Mech ability. You may gain cards directly to your hand instead of the active row. Gives more flexibility in timing when to play gained cards and helps build toward the 8+ cards glory category.
Starting character/companion pair. Akiko's character ability gains a face-up meteorite or Solves. Jiro's companion ability activates the previous card's ability or Vanquishes.
Starting character/companion pair. Gunter's character ability gains the core value of another controlled card or Vanquishes. Nacht is his companion.
Starting character/companion pair. Anna's character ability gains a face-up quest or Solves. Wojtek's companion ability rescues an active card or Vanquishes.
Starting character/companion pair. Matthew's character ability moves to an adjacent location or Vanquishes. Caesar is his companion.
Starting character/companion pair. Olga's character ability gains 1 exposed benefit from an adjacent location or Vanquishes. Changa is her companion.

Determined by the worker icon at the bottom-left corner of the card. The card's color indicates which worker color is required to activate its ability. The 5 colors are red, blue, green, yellow, and purple.

The 1x notation on certain benefit icons means you may only activate the selected card's ability once per turn. A card's ability may only be activated once per turn unless specifically noted otherwise.
The game end is triggered when any player places their 4th glory token via Boast. After this, every other player takes exactly one final turn. Then final scoring occurs.
Add up: all coins earned during the game + glory token values ($5/$6/$8/$10 each based on quests solved) + $2 per corruption token collected + coin values on upgraded items. Highest total wins.
Each glory token's end-game value depends on how many quests you solved: 0 quests = $5, 1 quest = $6, 2 quests = $8, 3+ quests = $10. This makes quest solving the single biggest scoring multiplier.
Each corruption token (both orange and turquoise) collected during the game is worth $2 at end of game. This includes corruption cards if playing with the Gears of Corruption expansion.
Each upgraded item card has a coin value printed on its bottom-right corner. This value is added to your final score.

If tied on total coins, add up all progress across the 8 glory categories (quests solved + meteorites melded + items upgraded + corruption collected + workers + maps + cards controlled). If still tied, share the victory.
